context.projectOverview: Calendar planner sub-system. Sprint 1 (DONE) built the pure deterministic slotter (src/calendar/slotter.ts planSlots), the local Finding consume-type and ProposedPlan/PlanItem/BusyInterval types (src/calendar/types.ts), JSON finding/freebusy readers (src/calendar/finding-source.ts), and the `bober calendar plan --dry-run` CLI (src/cli/commands/calendar.ts with extracted runCalendarPlan core + CalendarPlanDeps). This Sprint 2 introduces the connector abstraction and the first concrete connector: a local-first zero-egress .ics exporter, wired via `bober calendar plan --export-ics <path>`.

completedSprints.title: Deterministic slot-fill engine + dry-run plan CLI
completedSprints.summary: src/calendar/{types,slotter,finding-source}.ts + src/cli/commands/calendar.ts (runCalendarPlan core, CalendarPlanDeps injectable readFindings/readFreeBusy/nowIso). ProposedPlan = { scheduled: PlanItem[], unscheduled: {findingId, reason}[] }; PlanItem = { findingId, title, startIso, endIso }; BusyInterval = { startIso, endIso }. Slotter is strictly pure. commit 0d141c1.

relevantFiles: src/calendar/types.ts (REUSE PlanItem, ProposedPlan, BusyInterval, SlotConstraints — do not redefine)
src/calendar/slotter.ts (do NOT change the algorithm; Sprint 2 only adds connector + export path)
src/cli/commands/calendar.ts (extend runCalendarPlan to accept --export-ics <path> and call the connector via injectable deps; keep --dry-run path unchanged)
src/mcp/external-client.ts (reference for what NOT to import in ics-connector.ts — the .ics path must have ZERO network egress)
src/utils/fs.ts (writeJson/readJson helpers; for .ics use node:fs/promises writeFile directly with CRLF text)
.bober/principles.md (ESM .js imports, node:fs/promises only, type-only imports, unicode section headers)
